name: Sunglint

visual_attributes.morphology: Reflective surface pattern on ocean water
distinctive_features: Silvery mirror-like appearance on smooth water
Wave patterns indicating atmospheric gravity waves
visual_attributes.texture: Smooth and reflective on calm water, rough on disturbed water
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Varies depending on the area of ocean surface and angle of sunlight
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Alternating bright and dark regions corresponding to wave crests and troughs

domain_specific_visual.OCEAN_SURFACE: Reflective and smooth in sunglint areas
domain_specific_visual.ATMOSPHERIC_WAVES: Visible as patterns in sunglint regions

functional_attributes.primary_function: Reflects sunlight back to sensors, revealing ocean and atmospheric features
secondary_functions: Enhances visibility of ocean surface patterns

domain_specific_functional.SATELLITE_IMAGING: Enhances detection of oceanic and atmospheric phenomena

relationships.description: Sunglint is captured by satellites when sunlight reflects off the ocean surface.
Sunglint occurs due to sunlight reflecting at specific angles.

contextual_attributes.origin: Occurs naturally on ocean surfaces worldwide
contextual_attributes.historical_period: Observed since the advent of satellite imaging
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Global, wherever sunlight and ocean surfaces interact
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Used in scientific studies of ocean and atmospheric conditions

contrastive_constraints: NOT a static reflection without atmospheric influence
NOT limited to a specific geographic location
